content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter Monthly Report (April 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
Development activity for April 2026 centered on improving the developer experience and cross-platform accessibility of the `plugin-openrouter` repository. The primary focus was resolving environment-specific friction points that hindered Windows users. By addressing artifact-related blocking issues, the project continues to prioritize modularity and ease of integration for contributors across diverse operating systems.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Environment Compatibility
The primary technical effort this month was dedicated to ensuring a seamless setup process for Windows developers. 
* **Windows Git Compatibility:** A critical issue was identified where PGlite `:memory:` artifacts were interfering with git checkouts, preventing Windows users from successfully initializing the repository. This was addressed in [#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter/pull/25), which removes these artifacts to streamline the development environment.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
* **Closed Issues:** There were no issues formally closed during this reporting period.
* **New & Active Issues:** No new issues were opened in April. The project currently has one active pull request, [#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter/pull/25), which remains the primary focus for the maintainer team as it awaits final review. There are currently no other outstanding issues or blockers reported.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
Collaboration during April was focused on the technical resolution of environment-specific bugs. The submission of [#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter/pull/25) demonstrates a proactive approach to maintaining the project's accessibility for a broader range of contributors. As the PR is currently awaiting review, the community is focused on ensuring that the proposed fix meets the project's standards for stability and cross-platform compatibility.
